summary: This is a brief link post directing readers to Cathy O'Neil's Bloomberg column about Cambridge Analytica. The post references a Vice Motherboard article about Cambridge Analytica's role in Brexit and Trump's election, and links to her Bloomberg response titled 'Trump's Secret Sauce Is Just More Ketchup.' The dismissive title suggests O'Neil is skeptical of claims about Cambridge Analytica's revolutionary data capabilities.
key_points: O'Neil wrote a Bloomberg response to reporting about Cambridge Analytica's influence on Brexit and Trump
The headline 'Trump's Secret Sauce Is Just More Ketchup' signals skepticism about Cambridge Analytica's supposedly groundbreaking techniques
This post serves primarily as a pointer to her longer-form analysis elsewhere
money_quotes: Trump's 'Secret Sauce' Is Just More Ketchup
key_insight: O'Neil appears to view Cambridge Analytica's much-hyped data operation as more ordinary than revolutionary, suggesting the 'secret sauce' is overhyped marketing rather than breakthrough technology.
